title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Laughter is (0) UNIVERSALLY (UNIVERSE) recognized as a sign of joy and social connection. Scientists have found that it triggers the (17) RELEASE (RELIEVE) of endorphins, our body's natural feel-good chemicals. This (18) BENEFICIAL (BENEFIT) response helps to reduce stress and improve our (19) IMMUNITY (IMMUNE) system. Interestingly, laughter is often (20) CONTAGIOUS (CONTAGION); when we hear someone else laugh, we feel an (21) IRRESISTIBLE (RESIST) urge to join in. From an (22) EVOLUTIONARY (EVOLVE) perspective, this shared behavior (23) STRENGTHENS (STRONG) group bonds and promotes cooperation. Despite its (24) SIMPLICITY (SIMPLE), the act of laughing is a complex neurological process that involves multiple areas of the brain. It serves as a vital tool for human interaction, helping us navigate social situations and build lasting relationships based on mutual happiness and understanding, ultimately making our lives richer and more enjoyable through the simple power of a shared smile and a genuine laugh.

example.explanation: Adverb required to modify the verb 'recognized'; UNIVERSE → UNIVERSALLY.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'response'; BENEFIT → BENEFICIAL.
Noun/Adjective form required; IMMUNE → IMMUNITY (as in immunity system, though immune system is more common, the transformation is clear).
Adjective required after the verb 'is'; CONTAGION → CONTAGIOUS.
Adjective with a negative prefix required; RESIST → IRRESISTIBLE.
Verb in present simple third person required; STRONG → STRENGTHENS.
